sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, with month-specific anchors first where possible.

items.title: Resident Evil 4
items.body: Capcom remakes a canon object without treating it like glass.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists March 24.

items.title: Dredge
items.body: Fishing, inventory grids and cosmic dread become the year's quiet sea story.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists March 30.

items.title: Hi-Fi Rush
items.body: Tango Gameworks shadow-drops rhythm-action colour into a dark industry winter.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists January 25.

items.title: Dead Space
items.body: A 2008 horror object is restored for a new hardware generation.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists January 27.

items.title: Hogwarts Legacy
items.body: A huge licensed RPG becomes impossible to separate from online argument.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists February 10.

items.title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
items.body: Nintendo makes player invention feel like the headline feature.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists May 12.

items.title: Diablo IV
items.body: Blizzard's action RPG becomes a live-service ritual again.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists June 6.

items.title: Final Fantasy XVI
items.body: Square Enix gives Final Fantasy a darker action-RPG silhouette.
items.sourceNote: GameSpot 2023 calendar lists June 22.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: PlayStation VR2 launches
items.body: Sony's second VR headset arrives into a market that is curious but cautious.
items.sourceNote: Sony Interactive Entertainment confirms February 22, 2023 launch.

items.title: Microsoft completes the Activision Blizzard acquisition
items.body: The largest games acquisition story closes in October after regulatory drama.
items.sourceNote: Microsoft confirms October 13, 2023 completion.

items.title: Game Pass becomes a flagship release venue
items.body: Hi-Fi Rush, Starfield and The Finals show how storefront, subscription and surprise release can overlap.
items.sourceNote: 2023 release/platform context.

items.title: Switch reaches a late-generation peak
items.body: Tears of the Kingdom shows Nintendo's aging hardware can still command the calendar.
items.sourceNote: 2023 release and sales context; verify specific figures before final copy.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How players discovered, argued about and shared the month.

items.title: Spoiler etiquette becomes survival
items.body: Tears of the Kingdom builds, Baldur's Gate choices and Alan Wake twists make muting keywords a habit.

items.title: Shadow-drops feel magical
items.body: Hi-Fi Rush reminds players that surprise can still cut through a choreographed calendar.

items.title: CRPG discourse goes mainstream
items.body: Baldur's Gate 3 turns dice rolls, romances and character builds into social media weather.

items.title: Platform arguments get corporate
items.body: The Microsoft/Activision close makes business news feel inseparable from player identity.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A short atmospheric reading from the player side of recent history.

items.title: The year felt absurdly abundant
items.body: Zelda, Baldur's Gate, Starfield, Spider-Man and Alan Wake seemed too many for one calendar.

items.title: Old forms came back stronger
items.body: CRPGs, survival horror and 2D Mario all returned without feeling antique.

items.title: Players became inventors
items.body: Tears of the Kingdom and Baldur's Gate 3 made emergent stories feel like screenshots from friends.

items.title: Awards season felt obvious and impossible
items.body: Baldur's Gate 3 was the favourite, but the shelf behind it was unusually deep.
